arts.ttl: Coventry City v Tottenham live updates and team news from the CBS Arena
arts.sum: Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group. The two sides have met five times in the League Cup competition, with Spurs winning three times and the Sky Blues twice so far in the clubs' histories.

arts.ttl: Why there will be no VAR in Tottenham Carabao Cup clash vs Coventry City
arts.sum: Unlike in the Premier League, though, Spurs will have to face Coventry without VAR being used in the fixture. Tottenham Hotspur's quest to win a major trophy this season begins with the Carabao Cup tie against Coventry City.

arts.ttl: Jermaine Jenas decision made as former Tottenham star misses dream job audition
arts.sum: The 41-year-old had co-hosted the One Show alongside Alex Jones since 2020 and was part of BBC's football coverage on Match of the Day 2 and live studio games. Being a presenter for the BBC Champions League highlights show would have been a good audition for Jenas.

arts.ttl: 'Love watching them': Ange Postecoglou names five Spurs youngsters who've really impressed him in training
Ange Postecoglou explains why he loves watching Lucas Bergvall, Gray and Odobert in training
arts.sum: While all five teenagers come with bags of ability and excitement surrounding them, how does the Tottenham head coach marry the process of developing them all with the need to bring success to the club in the short-term and end that 16-year trophy drought at the north London outfit? Spurs had three new teenagers arrive into their first team this summer in Lucas Bergvall, Archie Gray and Wilson Odobert, signed for a combined £78.5million between them, while Yang Min-hyeok and Luka Vuskovic will join up Postecoglou's squad next year to continue the conveyor belt of talent coming into the club.

arts.ttl: A Tottenham therapy session: Ange Postecoglou, Spurs' tier-3 status and the battle of a lifetime
arts.sum: Speaking to the press post-match, Postecoglou cut a particularly grizzly figure, reasserting that he 'always' wins trophies in his second season with a club. After ten games of the 2023/24 Premier League season, Tottenham Hotspur had hailed Ange Postecoglou as their outback overlord.
